projects.title: An AI Study Partner for Students
projects.zh: 给同学用的 AI 学习助手
projects.summary: A small product experiment that turns mistakes into clearer, more useful review plans.
projects.short: Turning mistakes into smarter review plans.
projects.oneLine: Can AI help a student reflect—without doing the thinking for them?
projects.question: Most study tools give more answers. I wanted to explore whether an AI tool could help students notice patterns in their mistakes and decide what to review next. / 多数学习工具在提供更多答案，我想探索 AI 是否能帮助学生发现错题规律，并主动决定下一步复习什么。
process: Interviewed classmates about how they currently review mistakes. / 访谈同学现有的错题复习方式。
Mapped the smallest useful workflow: capture, classify, explain, plan. / 设计最小工作流。
Built and tested a lightweight conversational prototype. / 制作并测试对话原型。
projects.result: The prototype showed that classification alone was not useful enough. The most valuable moment was when the tool asked the student to explain why an answer was wrong before suggesting a plan.
projects.lessons: A good learning product should create productive friction. If AI removes every difficult step, it can also remove the moment where learning happens. / 好的学习产品需要保留有效的困难。
projects.nextTitle: Why Does Short-form Content Hold Attention?

projects.title: Why Does Short-form Content Hold Attention?
projects.zh: 短内容为什么能抓住注意力？
projects.summary: A media study of visual rhythm, narrative compression and audience behavior.
projects.short: A study of visual rhythm, story and attention.
projects.oneLine: Attention is shaped by hundreds of tiny creative decisions.
projects.question: Instead of asking whether short-form media is good or bad, I wanted to understand how it holds attention—and which techniques can be used without manipulating the audience. / 我没有先判断短内容好坏，而是研究它如何留住注意力。
process: Collected 30 successful videos across three topics. / 收集三个主题下的 30 个样本。
Coded hooks, cuts, information density and open loops. / 标记开头、剪辑与信息密度。
Re-edited one idea using three different rhythms. / 用三种节奏重新剪辑同一内容。
projects.result: The clearest pattern was not speed. Strong pieces created a question early, changed visual information when attention dipped, and delivered a satisfying answer without wasting time.
projects.lessons: Fast is not the same as clear. The best rhythm follows the idea. / 快不等于清楚，节奏应该服务于想法。

projects.title: My First ¥100 from an Idea
projects.zh: 第一次从想法赚到 100 元
projects.summary: A tiny business experiment in finding a real need, delivering value and asking for payment.
projects.short: A tiny experiment in finding and delivering value.
projects.oneLine: Revenue is evidence that someone valued the result—not proof that the idea was perfect.
projects.question: Could I earn a small amount by solving a real problem, without relying on luck, reselling or asking friends to support me? / 我能否通过解决一个真实问题获得第一笔收入？
process: Listed problems I could solve with skills I already had. / 列出现有能力能解决的问题。
Talked to potential users before building anything. / 先访谈，再制作。
Offered one clear service with a fixed scope and price. / 提供范围与价格明确的服务。
projects.result: The first offer was too broad. After narrowing the outcome and showing a sample, the value became easier to understand. One real transaction taught me more than ten imagined business plans.
projects.lessons: People do not buy effort; they buy a result they understand. / 用户购买的不是努力，而是能够理解的结果。

projects.title: 30 Days of Thinking in English
projects.zh: 连续 30 天用英语思考
projects.summary: A daily challenge to express one original thought in English without drafting it in Chinese first.
projects.short: One original thought in English, every day.
projects.oneLine: Fluency grows when English becomes a place to think, not a code to translate.
projects.question: I knew many words but still translated sentences in my head. Would writing one small original idea every day make English feel more direct? / 每天直接用英语写一个想法，会发生什么？
process: Wrote for ten minutes without using a translator. / 每天十分钟，不使用翻译器。
Marked repeated gaps after writing, not during it. / 写完后再记录表达缺口。
Rewrote one entry each week for clarity. / 每周重写一篇。
projects.result: The entries did not become perfect, but they became faster and more personal. Repeated sentence patterns revealed exactly what I needed to learn next.
projects.output: 30 bilingual entries + a personal phrase bank
projects.lessons: Accuracy matters, but hesitation can become its own habit. Create first, correct second. / 准确很重要，但犹豫也会成为习惯。
